title: Disorders of the Thyroid Gland
authors: Grace Kim, Debika Nandi-Munshi & Carolina Cecilia Di Blasi

blocks.text:
ทำไม neonatologist ต้องเข้าใจ Disorders of the Thyroid Gland?
Thyroid hormone เป็น critical driver ของ neurodevelopment ในปีแรกของชีวิต — hypothyroidism ที่ไม่ได้รับการรักษาคือ most common preventable cause of intellectual disability. สิ่งสำคัญ 6 ข้อ: (1) CH incidence = 1:2,500–3,000 — พบบ่อยกว่า phenylketonuria; (2) NBS ด้วย TSH+T4 = ideal; primary TSH alone พลาด central CH + TBG deficiency; (3) Preterm = ลำบาก: TSH surge ต่ำ → false-negative NBS; ต้อง repeat screen; (4) Wolff-Chaikoff effect ยังไม่ mature ใน preterm → iodine-containing disinfectants contraindicated; (5) Neonatal Graves disease = transient แต่อันตราย: tachycardia + heart failure + death ถ้า treat ช้า; (6) LT4 10–15 μg/kg/day, start ≤ 2 สัปดาห์ — ยิ่งเร็วยิ่งดี, target T4 10–16 μg/dL upper half of normal range.
เป้าหมายการเรียนรู้วันนี้
อธิบาย HPT axis feedback loop และ thyroid hormone synthesis ครบ 7 steps
รู้ fetal-placental thyroid interaction: อะไรข้าม placenta ได้บ้าง (T4 ได้, TSH ไม่ได้); ผลของ TRBAbs vs TSI ต่อ neonate
อธิบาย postnatal TSH surge: timing, mechanism, และความแตกต่างระหว่าง term vs preterm
รู้ epidemiology + pathophysiology ของ CH: dysgenesis (85%), dyshormonogenesis (10–15%), transient, central
แปลผล NBS screening ได้: 6 pattern (Low T4+High TSH, Low T4+Normal TSH, Normal T4+High TSH, etc.) + algorithm
รู้ specific mutations: NKX2.1 (brain-lung-thyroid), FOXE1 (Bamforth-Lazarus), PAX8, TPO — phenotype ของแต่ละตัว
วินิจฉัย central hypothyroidism: biochemical profile + syndromic associations (septo-optic dysplasia, panhypopituitarism)
รู้ thyroid problems ใน Down syndrome: prevalence, subclinical vs overt CH, TRBAb-induced transient CH
อธิบาย consumptive hypothyroidism จาก hepatic hemangioma: mechanism (type 3 DI ↑), treatment (LT4+LT3)
รู้ transient hypothyroxinemia of prematurity: etiology, evidence ของ LT4 replacement (controversy)
รักษา CH ได้: LT4 dose, target, monitoring schedule (Box 86.1), and liquid vs tablet formulation
วินิจฉัย + รักษา neonatal Graves disease: methimazole, Lugol's solution, propranolol, dose, and resolution timeline
1. Regulation of Thyroid Function — HPT Axis
1.1 Feedback Loop
Hypothalamus → TRH → anterior pituitary → TSH → thyroid → T3 + T4 → negative feedback ที่ pituitary + hypothalamus
Logarithmic relationship fT4–TSH: small changes in fT4 → large changes in TSH; ดังนั้น TSH = most sensitive indicator ของ thyroid status
T3 levels ใน pituitary กำหนด TSH secretion; T3 + T4 feedback ที่ pituitary; TRH determines setpoint
Iodine-thyroid relationship: ↑↑ iodine → ↓ T4 synthesis transiently (Wolff-Chaikoff effect) → escape ใน ~48 hr ใน term infant
1.2 Thyroid Hormone Synthesis — 7 Steps

rows: Oxidation I⁻ → I₂ at apical membrane
TPO: MIT+DIT → T3; DIT+DIT → T4 (stored in TG colloid)
Endocytosis of TG into follicular cell
Hydrolysis: T4 + T3 released into circulation
Deiodination MIT + DIT → recycle I⁻ + tyrosine

blocks.text: 1.3 Serum Protein Binding
Primary transport: TBG ~75%; TBPA (transthyretin) ~12.5%; albumin ~12.5%
T4 : T3 ratio in serum = 50–100 : 1; thyroid gland = only source of T4
fT4 = gold standard — only unbound hormone enters cells; total T4 misleading ถ้า TBG ผิดปกติ
TBG เพิ่มขึ้นใน late gestation (stimulated by estrogen); gene location: X chromosome → X-linked TBG deficiency
2. Fetal Thyroid Development & Maturation
2.1 Embryogenesis of Thyroid
Thyroid = first endocrine organ to develop; visible by day 22 (median anlage from pharyngeal pouch floor + bilateral ultimobranchial bodies)
Migration days 32–48 → pretracheal region; by 12 weeks: iodide uptake, TBG synthesis, H₂O₂ production → T4 detected in fetal blood by 11–12 weeks gestation
Key transcription factors: NKX2.1 (TTF-1), FOXE1 (TTF-2), PAX8 — mutations → thyroid dysgenesis; see Table 86.3
TSH detectable at 10–12 weeks gestation; pituitary mature by 14–15 weeks
2.2 Genes and Thyroid Phenotype — Table 86.3

rows: Cleft palate, choanal atresia, kinky hair, bifid epiglottis → Bamforth-Lazarus syndrome
Respiratory distress syndrome, choreoathetosis, hypotonia → Brain-lung-thyroid syndrome
Cysts in thyroid remnants, kidney/urinary tract malformations

blocks.text: 2.3 Fetal-Placental-Maternal Thyroid Interaction
Crosses placenta freely: iodide, TRH, antithyroid drugs (PTU/MMI), TRBAbs, TSI (IgG) → affect fetal thyroid
Crosses placenta partially: T4 (critical in first trimester before fetal axis matures); T3 probably does NOT cross significantly
Does NOT cross: TSH
Vulsma et al. NEJM 1989: T4 does cross placenta — athyreotic fetuses had T4 levels 25–50% of normal term; landmark study proving maternal T4 essential for early fetal brain
hCG (from placenta) has TSH-like activity → ↑ maternal T4 in first trimester + ↓ TSH; TBG ↑ from estrogen
Placenta type 3 DI activity = 200× type 2 DI; converts T4 → rT3, T3 → DIT → maintains low fetal T3 (protective until term)
2.4 Deiodinases — D1, D2, D3

rows: Outer ring → T4 to T3 (activation); inner ring → T4 to rT3 (inactivation)
Nonselective; inhibited by PTU + rT3; quiescent until late gestation → late rise in fetal T3
Outer ring only → T4 to T3 (activation)
Brain, pituitary, placenta, brown adipose, heart, thyroid
NOT inhibited by PTU; inhibited by thyroid hormone; ↑ in hypothyroid brain → preserve local T3
Inner ring → T4 to rT3, T3 to DIT (both inactive)
Dominant in fetal tissues; hemangiomas ↑↑ D3 → consumptive hypothyroidism

blocks.text: 2.5 Postnatal Thyroid Adaptation — TSH Surge
At birth: fT4 slightly below maternal; T3 low in cord blood
TSH surge: peaks 2–4 hr of life, returns to baseline ~48 hr; response to cold + stress
T3 + T4 peak by 24 hr of life; progressively decrease over 4–5 weeks
TSH at 1 month: ~5 mU/L; near-adult range 0.5–4 mU/L by 2nd month
Preterm TSH surge: attenuated (immature HPT axis); T3 + T4 rise blunted; TSH peak ~40 mU/L at 30 min (lower than term); high rT3 levels

NBS Timing Rule
Screen 2–4 days of age (optimal). < 48 hr → ↑ false-positive จาก normal physiologic TSH surge. Screen < 24 hr → must repeat at 2 weeks. NICU/preterm: screen within 7 days of birth + second screen at 2–6 weeks (HPT axis matures 2–6 weeks; delayed TSH rise may be missed on first screen).
3. Congenital Hypothyroidism (CH)
3.1 Epidemiology
Incidence: 1:2,500–3,000; central CH = 1:80,000–1:100,000 (Netherlands data: 1:21,000)
Female : Male = 2 : 1; higher prevalence in Asian, Hispanic; lower in Black; ↑ risk in Down syndrome (14–21× general population), macrosomic (>4,500 g), LBW (<2,000 g)
Delays in treating CH = most common preventable cause of intellectual disability
3.2 Pathophysiology — Causes of CH (Table 86.1)

rows: Agenesis; ectopy (2/3 of cases, lingual/neck/substernal); hypoplasia — ectopy = female predominant
TPO defect (most common), NIS defect, DuOX2 defect, TG defect, Pendred (SLC26A4), dehalogenase defect
Maternal TRBAbs, antithyroid drugs, iodine excess/deficiency, heterozygous DUOXA2 mutations
Panhypopituitarism, isolated TSH deficiency, PROP1/LHX3/LHX4/POU1F1/HESX1 mutations

blocks.text: 3.3 Dyshormonogenesis — Specific Defects
TPO defect: most common dyshormonogenesis; autosomal recessive; permanent goitrous CH; high TG; high uptake on scintigraphy
DuOX2 (dual oxidase 2) defect: H₂O₂ generation at apical membrane; heterozygous → transient CH; homozygous → permanent
TG defect: TG gene chr 8; low/undetectable TG + goiter + CH at birth; AR

Pendred syndrome: SLC26A4 gene (pendrin, chloride-iodide transporter) on chr 7; partial organification defect + goiter + sensorineural deafness; expressed in cochlea, thyroid, kidney; mild thyroid phenotype, seldom detected by NBS TSH
Dehalogenase defect: MIT + DIT in urine (hallmark); iodine wasting → goiter + CH
NIS defect (SLC5A5): goiter present but ↓/absent uptake on scintigraphy (paradox — uptake test measures NIS function)
3.4 Clinical Presentation of CH
First 0–7 days (often subtle): prolonged jaundice >3 days (73%), BW >4 kg (40%), poor feeding (40%), transient hypothermia (38%), large posterior fontanel >5 mm (32%)
1–4 weeks: failure to gain weight (45%), constipation (35%), hypoactivity (33%)
1–3 months: failure to thrive (90%), umbilical hernia (49%), macroglossia (43%), myxedema (40%), hoarse cry (30%)
Classical facies: depressed nasal bridge, narrow forehead, puffy eyelids; protuberant tongue; hoarse cry (myxedema vocal cords)
Cardiac: enlarged silhouette + low-voltage ECG + prolonged conduction; goiter may be present

⚠️ อาการ CH มักยังไม่ชัดใน 0–2 สัปดาห์แรก
ทารก athyreotic ยังได้รับ maternal T4 ~1/3 ข้าม placenta ก่อนคลอด — half-life T4 = 6 days → หมดใน 3–4 สัปดาห์หลังคลอด. ดังนั้น classic signs อาจยังไม่ปรากฏจนกว่า weeks 6–12. NBS คือสิ่งเดียวที่ detect ได้ก่อนอาการ — นี่คือเหตุผลหลักที่ทำ NBS.
4. NBS Screening + Algorithm Interpretation
4.1 Screening Methods

rows: TBG deficiency; hypothalamic-pituitary CH; hypothyroxinemia with delayed TSH rise
Most common method globally; use TSH cutoff 20–25 mU/L at 24 hr for better sensitivity
Delayed TSH elevation with initially normal T4
IDEAL approach — recommended by van Trotsenburg Thyroid 2021

blocks.text: 5–8% ของ affected infants escape detection by NBS: delayed TSH elevation, sample collection errors, or central CH (normal TSH)
Second screen recommended for: preterm, LBW/VLBW, ill neonates, screen before 24 hr, multiple births (twin-to-twin dilution)
4.2 Interpreting NBS Results — Key Cutoffs
TSH >40 μU/L after 24 hr of life = highly suggestive of primary CH → confirmatory serum fT4 + TSH immediately → start LT4
TSH 20–40 mU/L = indeterminate (8–10% of CH infants) → repeat screen ASAP + serum fT4/T4/TSH
Normal T4 + ↑ TSH at 2–4 weeks follow-up: consider transient/mild CH, delayed maturation HPT axis, Down syndrome
Low T4 + normal TSH: central hypothyroidism vs TBG deficiency → measure fT4 + TBG to distinguish: low TBG + normal fT4 = TBG deficiency (euthyroid); low fT4 + normal TBG = central CH
Normal TSH at 2–12 weeks = 9.1 mU/L

Central CH — อย่าพลาดเพราะรอ TSH สูง
Central CH: TSH normal หรือต่ำ + T4 ต่ำ + fT4 ต่ำ. NBS programs ที่ใช้ TSH-elevation เป็น trigger จะ MISS central CH ทุกราย. Red flags: midline facial defects + hypoglycemia + microphallus + nystagmus + polyuria → สงสัย panhypopituitarism. ใน central CH: ตรวจ adrenal function ก่อน start LT4 — เพราะ LT4 เพิ่ม cortisol metabolism → อาจ precipitate adrenal crisis.
5. Special Hypothyroid Entities
5.1 Down Syndrome Thyroid
CH prevalence in Down syndrome: 28–35× higher than general population; estimate 1:50–1:100 Down syndrome infants
Most common: thyroid hypoplasia; agenesis/ectopy rare. TSH generally in highest percentiles at fetal thyroid exam
Subclinical hypothyroidism (TSH 6–10 mU/L, normal T4) frequent — Claret et al. Pediatr Res 2013: 73.6% resolve spontaneously; higher remission if no autoantibodies + no goiter

Evidence to treat subclinical CH in Down: improved growth (Sharav AJDC 1988) + better cognitive outcomes (van Trotsenburg JCEM 2005). Recommendation: measure TSH at end of neonatal period in all Down syndrome infants.
Hyperthyroidism in Down: rare in neonatal period (most common in adolescence); prevalence 0–3%
5.2 Consumptive Hypothyroidism
Mechanism: hepatic hemangiomas (infantile) → massive ↑ type 3 DI (MDI III) → accelerates T4→rT3 + T3→T2 (both inactive) → profound hypothyroidism
Huang SA et al. NEJM 2000;343:185: landmark paper — severe hypothyroidism + markedly elevated rT3 + suppressed T3+T4 in infant with hepatic hemangiomas
Labs: low T3 + T4, elevated TSH, elevated rT3 — rT3 elevation = hallmark; refractory to standard LT4 doses
Treatment: very high-dose LT4 + LT3 (liothyronine); some respond to propranolol alone (Osada 2019); regression of hemangioma → normalization
Other causes: cutaneous hemangiomas, gastrointestinal stromal tumors (GIST) with paraneoplastic type 3 DI production
5.3 Transient Primary Hypothyroidism
Causes: excess iodine (Wolff-Chaikoff), maternal antithyroid drugs, maternal TRBAbs, heterozygous DUOXA2 mutations, large hepatic hemangiomas

TRBAb (TSH receptor-blocking antibody) mechanism: IgG crosses placenta after 16 weeks → blocks TSH receptor in neonatal thyroid → transient CH; does NOT affect thyroid embryogenesis (แค่ block function ชั่วคราว); duration correlates with maternal antibody titer
TRBAb thyroid scan: may mimic agenesis (TSH-induced uptake blocked) → don't rely on scan alone; confirm with TRBAb titer in maternal + neonatal serum
Treatment: LT4 ถ้า TSH still elevated at 2 weeks; continue until age 3 years → trial off LT4

Wolff-Chaikoff in preterm: does NOT mature until end of third trimester → iodine-containing disinfectants (povidone-iodine) contraindicated in preterm; Aitken & Williams Arch Dis Child Fetal Neonatal Ed 2014: systematic review confirms topical iodine → transient thyroid dysfunction
5.4 Euthyroid Sick Syndrome
Acutely/chronically ill infants + children: ↓ T3 activation peripherally → low T3, ↑ rT3, TSH inappropriately low or normal; T4 low or normal; fT4 may be normal
Common in: preterm with RDS, severe gastroenteritis, acute leukemia, cardiac surgery (↑ rT3 within 2 hr post-op, precedes ↓ T3)
Treatment NOT indicated — reverses as primary illness resolves; TSH may transiently ↑ up to 15 mU/L during recovery
Preterm: physiologically low T4 + fT4 + T3 + high rT3 → hard to distinguish euthyroid sick from true hypothyroid in sick preterm — context + trend มากกว่า single value
5.5 Transient Hypothyroxinemia of Prematurity (THOP)
Incidence: common in preterm <32 weeks; degree of hypothyroxinemia inversely proportional to GA
Etiology: loss of maternal T4 transfer at delivery + HPT axis immaturity + iodine deficiency + nonthyroidal illness + ↓ TBG (undernutrition/hepatic dysfunction) + dopamine infusion + glucocorticoids
Labs: low T4, low fT4, normal or low TSH (not elevated — key difference from primary CH)

LT4 replacement: controversial; Osborn DA Cochrane 2001 (CD001070): LT4 for preterm hypothyroxinemia — no consistent improvement in neurology or mortality; current recommendation: treat only if low T4 accompanied by TSH elevation (van Trotsenburg 2021 consensus)
Dopamine infusion (>2 μg/kg/min) → inhibits TSH secretion; glucocorticoids → suppress TSH; blood transfusion → exogenous T4 → ↓ TSH
6. Thyroid Hormone Carrier Protein Disorders
6.1 TBG Deficiency
Prevalence: 1:2,500–1:12,000; X-linked → males 1:2,400–1:2,800 (complete); females = partial
Clinical: euthyroid — no clinical significance; total T4 low, fT4 normal, TSH normal; T3RU elevated (indirect TBG measure)
Distinguish from central CH: TBG deficiency → low total T4 + normal fT4 + low TBG + normal TSH; central CH → low total T4 + low fT4 + normal TBG + normal/low TSH
Treatment: none required; 26 different mutations reported (missense, deletion, truncation)
6.2 TBG Excess & Familial Dysalbuminemic Hyperthyroxinemia (FDH)
TBG excess: 1:15,000–1:25,000; X-linked; ↑ total T4 + mild ↑ T3, normal TSH + fT4; euthyroid; no treatment
FDH: ALB gene mutations (autosomal dominant); albumin affinity for T4 ↑ 60×; ↑ total T4, normal fT4 + TSH; euthyroid; falsely elevated fT4 on many immunoassays
FDH diagnosis: protein electrophoresis with labeled T4; or free thyroxine index method (Refetoff Thyroid 2020); no treatment
7. Treatment of Congenital Hypothyroidism
7.1 When to Start LT4
Start LT4 if: fT4 low + TSH elevated; OR TSH >20 mU/L even if fT4 normal; OR TSH >6 mU/L beyond 21 days of age
Start ≤ 2 weeks of life; neurodevelopmental benefit of rapid normalization: target T4/fT4 within 3 days of treatment initiation (Salerno Thyroid 2002; Selva J Pediatr 2002)
In central CH: confirm intact adrenal function first before starting LT4
7.2 LT4 Dosing and Targets

rows: Higher dose (15 μg/kg) for severe disease (very low T4/fT4)

blocks.text: Formulation: tablet form preferred; crush + small liquid. FDA-approved liquid (Tirosint-SOL 2017): convenience, BUT 2 European studies: overtreatment (TSH suppression) with liquid vs tablet at equivalent weight-based dosing (Cherella Curr Opin Endocrinol 2020)
Avoid concomitant: soy, fiber, calcium, iron — all reduce LT4 absorption
7.3 Monitoring Schedule — Box 86.1

rows: History + PE; refer pediatric endocrinologist; recheck serum TSH + fT4; thyroid U/S ± scan
Trial off LT4 × 4–6 weeks; ↑ TSH = permanent CH

blocks.text:
Overtreatment Signs — ต้องรู้
LT4 มากเกินไป → tachycardia, irritability, disturbed sleep, excessive nervousness (thyrotoxicosis symptoms). Long-term overtreatment: premature synostosis ของ cranial sutures + advanced bone age. Monitor growth curve + labs regularly. Dose requirements ↓ rapidly after 2 weeks — close follow-up สำคัญมาก โดยเฉพาะในเดือนแรก.
8. Neonatal Hyperthyroidism (Graves Disease)
8.1 Epidemiology & Mechanism
1% of offspring born to mothers with Graves disease develop neonatal hyperthyroidism; overall incidence low
TSH receptor-stimulating antibodies (TSA/TSI) cross placenta → stimulate fetal + neonatal thyroid; NOT related to maternal thyroid hormone transfer
Fetal TSA levels ≈ maternal levels at 30 weeks gestation; fetal tachycardia + goiter + IUGR in third trimester
Fetal TSA >250% of upper limit → ↑ risk for thyrotoxicosis → monitor closely
Transient: resolves by 3–12 weeks (usual) / by 4 months (maximum) as maternal antibodies cleared
Rare permanent neonatal hyperthyroidism: activating TSH receptor mutations (McCune-Albright G protein mutation) → requires ablative therapy
8.2 Clinical Presentation
Classic triad: irritability + tachycardia + thyroid enlargement
Full picture: flushing, diarrhea, vomiting, hypertension, poor weight gain, exophthalmos; thrombocytopenia, hepatosplenomegaly, jaundice, hyperviscosity
Onset may be delayed 8–14 days: (1) depletion of maternal antithyroid drugs (PTU/MMI given to mother) postnatal; (2) maternal TRBAbs initially blocking TSI effect for several weeks → delayed onset
Severe untreated: arrhythmias + congestive heart failure + death
8.3 Management of Neonatal Graves Disease
Confirm diagnosis: elevated T4, fT4, T3 + suppressed TSH in postnatal blood (cord may be normal) at 2–5 days; measure maternal TSI/TRBAbs

rows: Drug of choice for neonatal hyperthyroidism
Rapidly inhibits thyroid hormone RELEASE (not synthesis)
5% iodine + 10% KI; use adjunctively for acute control
For tachycardia + hypertension; therapeutic response 24–36 hr
For severe thyrotoxicosis refractory to above

blocks.text: PTU: NOT recommended in children — Rivkees SA & Mattison DR NEJM 2009;360:1574: ↑ rates of PTU-induced liver failure in children; MMI preferred
Breastfeeding: safe — MMI concentration in breast milk very low; can breastfeed while on antithyroid drugs
Fetal thyrotoxicosis (in utero): treat by giving antithyroid drugs to mother; PTU preferred in first trimester (lower fetal malformation risk vs MMI)
If satisfactory response not seen in 24–36 hr: increase MMI + Lugol's dose by 50%
9. NICU Practical — Thyroid ที่ Bedside
NBS positive (↑ TSH) → NICU action: ส่ง confirmatory serum fT4 + TSH + refer pediatric endocrinologist; start LT4 ≤ 48 hr ไม่รอ endocrine callback ถ้า TSH >40 mU/L + low fT4
Preterm NICU thyroid monitoring: repeat NBS at 2–6 weeks; monitor fT4 + TSH serially ถ้ามี dopamine infusion, steroid, blood transfusion, iodine exposure
Povidone-iodine ใน preterm: ห้ามใช้เป็น skin disinfectant — Wolff-Chaikoff ยังไม่ mature → transient hypo/hyperthyroidism; use chlorhexidine แทน
Down syndrome: ตรวจ TSH ตอนจบ neonatal period; ถ้า TSH >10 mU/L ± low T4 → treat; subclinical TSH 6–10 mU/L → recheck 2–4 weeks; อย่า overtreated แต่อย่า undertreated
Hepatic hemangioma + hypothyroidism: ต้องสงสัย consumptive hypothyroidism ถ้ามี liver hemangioma + ↑ rT3 + refractory to standard LT4; add LT3 (liothyronine) and/or propranolol; endocrine consult

Maternal Graves disease history → neonatal monitoring: check maternal TSI/TRBAb level; examine neonate for goiter + tachycardia + exophthalmos; if TSI >250% ULN → close monitoring; onset may be delayed 8–14 days postpartum (maternal antithyroid drug depletion)
Central CH clues: midline defects + hypoglycemia + microphallus + nystagmus → check GH + cortisol + LH/FSH; septo-optic dysplasia on MRI; start LT4 only after cortisol/ACTH confirmed adequate
LT4 administration tip: crush tablet + suspend in few drops breast milk/formula; avoid soy formula, calcium-fortified formula, iron supplements within 2–4 hr — all reduce absorption
10. 5 Bedside Pearls — สำหรับพรุ่งนี้

PEARL 1 — TSH >40 mU/L หลัง 24 hr = Start LT4 ไม่รอ
NBS TSH >40 μU/L หลัง 24 hr ของชีวิต = primary CH highly likely. ส่ง confirmatory serum fT4+TSH ทันที แต่ start LT4 10–15 μg/kg/day ไม่รอ endo callback ถ้า clinical picture และ lab confirm. Target: T4 upper half of normal range ภายใน 3 วัน. ทุกวันที่ delay = neuron ที่สูญเสียไป.

PEARL 2 — Wolff-Chaikoff ยังไม่ mature ใน Preterm = No Povidone-Iodine
Term infant: Wolff-Chaikoff effect matures by end of third trimester → thyroid can 'escape' from iodine overload in ~48 hr. Preterm < 34 weeks: ยัง immature → iodine overload → transient hypo หรือ hyperthyroidism. Skin disinfectant สำหรับ procedure ใน preterm: ใช้ chlorhexidine เท่านั้น ห้ามใช้ betadine/povidone-iodine.

PEARL 3 — Consumptive Hypo: ↑↑ rT3 = Tip-off
Infant ที่มี hepatic hemangioma + hypothyroidism ที่ refractory ต่อ standard LT4 dose: ตรวจ rT3. ถ้า rT3 สูงมาก (+ low T3) = consumptive hypothyroidism จาก type 3 DI ใน hemangioma. Need very high-dose LT4 + add LT3 (liothyronine); propranolol ช่วยได้บาง case (Huang SA NEJM 2000;343:185). Resolves with hemangioma regression.

PEARL 4 — Neonatal Graves: Late Onset ถึง 2 สัปดาห์
Neonate ของแม่ Graves disease: อาการ อาจเริ่มช้าถึง 8–14 วันหลังคลอด เพราะ (1) maternal antithyroid drugs ยังค้างอยู่ใน neonate; (2) maternal TRBAb blocking TSA effect ชั่วคราว. ดังนั้น ต้องติดตาม neonate อย่างน้อย 2 สัปดาห์. Signs: tachycardia HR >160 + ผอมหน้า + goiter + exophthalmos → ส่ง T4/fT4/T3/TSH + start MMI.

PEARL 5 — Central CH: ตรวจ Adrenal ก่อน Start LT4
Central CH มาจาก pituitary/hypothalamic disease → มักมี panhypopituitarism ร่วมด้วย (GH ↓, ACTH ↓, gonadotropin ↓, ADH ↓). LT4 เพิ่ม cortisol metabolism → อาจ precipitate adrenal crisis ถ้า ACTH/cortisol insufficient. Rule: ยืนยัน adrenal function ก่อน start LT4 ใน central CH ทุกราย; ถ้าไม่แน่ใจ → stress-dose hydrocortisone cover ก่อน.
11. Evidence Update — Landmark Studies
Vulsma T et al. NEJM 1989;321:13: T4 crosses placenta to athyreotic fetuses — cord blood T4 = 25–50% of normal; landmark proving maternal T4 critical for early fetal neurodevelopment.
Rose SR et al. Pediatrics 2006;117:2290 (AAP/ATA Update): NBS algorithm for CH; combined T4+TSH = ideal method; timing and repeat screen recommendations; still widely cited as algorithm standard.

van Trotsenburg P et al. Thyroid 2021;31:387 (ENDO-ERN 2020–2021 consensus): Comprehensive CH guidelines update — screening methods, TBG/central CH differentiation, Down syndrome monitoring, fetal goiter management, liquid vs tablet formulations.
Salerno M et al. Thyroid 2002;12:45: High initial LT4 dose (50 μg/day) vs lower: higher dose → faster T4 normalization + better IQ at 4 years (IQ 98 vs 90); supports aggressive early treatment.

Lazarus JH et al. NEJM 2012;366:493 (CATS trial): Antenatal thyroid screening + LT4 for hypothyroxinemia — no cognitive benefit in offspring at 3 yr; suggests mild maternal hypothyroxinemia may not require treatment.
Casey BM et al. NEJM 2017;376:815: LT4 for subclinical hypothyroidism/hypothyroxinemia in pregnancy — no improvement in cognitive outcomes in children; controversial but practice-changing.

Huang SA et al. NEJM 2000;343:185: Severe hypothyroidism from type 3 deiodinase in infantile hemangiomas — first case series establishing consumptive hypothyroidism; confirms massively elevated rT3 as biomarker.
Rivkees SA & Mattison DR. NEJM 2009;360:1574: PTU-induced liver failure in children — proposes ending PTU use in pediatric patients; methimazole should be preferred drug.

Osborn DA. Cochrane Database Syst Rev 2001;4:CD001070: Thyroid hormones for preventing neurodevelopmental impairment in preterm infants — no consistent benefit from LT4 supplementation in THOP; evidence insufficient for routine use.

Aitken J & Williams FL. Arch Dis Child Fetal Neonatal Ed 2014;99:F21: Systematic review: topical iodine in preterm neonates → thyroid dysfunction confirmed; supports chlorhexidine as preferred disinfectant.
Claret C et al. Pediatr Res 2013;73:674: Subclinical hypothyroidism in Down syndrome — 73.6% resolved spontaneously; higher remission without thyroid autoantibodies.
van Trotsenburg AS et al. JCEM 2005;90:3304: RCT: LT4 in neonatal period for Down syndrome — improved development + growth at 2 years; supports early treatment of overt CH.
Brown RS et al. JCEM 1996;81:1147: TRBAb-induced transient CH in >1 million babies — 2% of CH infants; severity correlates with antibody titer.
Banige M et al. J Pediatr 2018;197:249: Prediction of neonatal hyperthyroidism — fetal TSA levels ≈ maternal at 30 weeks; TSA >250% ULN = increased risk; monitor closely.
12. Self-Check Questions พร้อมเฉลย
Q: NBS ขึ้น TSH 55 mU/L (24 hr of life) ใน term newborn — step ต่อไปคืออะไร? เริ่ม LT4 เลยได้เลยไหม?

ตอบ: TSH >40 μU/L หลัง 24 hr = primary CH highly likely. Action: ส่ง confirmatory serum fT4 + TSH ทันที (ไม่รอ). Start LT4 10–15 μg/kg/day ทันที — ไม่จำเป็นต้องรอ confirm ถ้า clinical + NBS consistent; การ delay มีผลต่อ neurodevelopment. Refer pediatric endocrinologist. Thyroid U/S ± scan ช่วย anatomic diagnosis แต่ไม่ delay treatment.
Q: Preterm 28 weeks อายุ 3 weeks: TSH 5 mU/L, fT4 0.7 ng/dL (low) — diagnose? รักษาไหม?

ตอบ: Pattern นี้ = transient hypothyroxinemia of prematurity (THOP): low fT4 + normal/low TSH. ไม่ใช่ primary CH (primary CH: TSH ↑↑). Causes: HPT immaturity + loss of maternal T4 + dopamine/steroids + iodine deficiency. Evidence (Osborn Cochrane 2001): LT4 ไม่ได้ improve neurology consistently. Current recommendation: treat ONLY if low T4 + TSH elevation; otherwise monitor. ถ้า TSH ยัง elevated นาน >1 เดือน + persistent low T4 → consider LT4 4–5 μg/kg/day.
Q: แยก TBG deficiency จาก central hypothyroidism ด้วยอะไร? lab pattern?

ตอบ: ทั้งสอง: low total T4 + normal TSH. แยกด้วย fT4 + TBG levels: TBG deficiency → low TBG + normal fT4 + normal TSH → euthyroid; Central CH → normal TBG + low fT4 + normal/low TSH → hypothyroid. Central CH ต้องตรวจ pituitary function ทั้งหมด (GH, ACTH, ADH, gonadotropin); TBG deficiency ไม่ต้องรักษา. X-linked → males complete deficiency, females partial.
Q: Transient neonatal hypothyroidism จาก TRBAbs — thyroid scan แสดงอะไร? pitfall?

ตอบ: TRBAbs block TSH receptor → block TSH-induced iodide uptake → scan อาจแสดง absent uptake ที่คล้าย thyroid agenesis. Pitfall: อย่าวินิจฉัย agenesis จาก scan เพียงอย่างเดียว ใน context ของ maternal Graves disease / autoimmune. Confirm ด้วย maternal + neonatal TRBAb titers. TRBAbs ≠ thyroid absence — ต้องรักษาชั่วคราว (6 months max); antibodies cross placenta after 16 weeks → do NOT affect thyroid embryogenesis → no permanent structural defect.
Q: Neonatal Graves disease: อาการอาจ delay ทำไม? management steps?

ตอบ: Delay reasons: (1) maternal antithyroid drugs (PTU/MMI) ที่ neonate ได้ transplacentally ต้อง clear ก่อน (half-life PTU ~1 hr, MMI ~6 hr → ใน neonate อาจช้ากว่า); (2) maternal TRBAbs blocking TSI effect ชั่วคราวสัปดาห์แรก → late onset. Management: methimazole 0.25–1 mg/kg/day ÷ q8h + Lugol's 1 drop TID + propranolol 2 mg/kg/day; monitor cardiac; ถ้าไม่ response 24–36 hr → เพิ่ม dose 50%; PTU ห้ามใช้ในเด็ก; resolves 3–12 weeks. Monitor closely for CHF.
Q: Consumptive hypothyroidism จาก hepatic hemangioma: mechanism + labs + treatment?

ตอบ: Mechanism: hemangioma tissue มี massive overexpression of type 3 deiodinase (MDI III) → accelerates T4 → rT3 + T3 → T2 (inactive); rate ของ thyroid hormone degradation เกิน production capacity. Labs: low T3 + T4, elevated TSH, elevated rT3 — rT3 สูงมาก = hallmark. Refractory to standard LT4 dose เพราะ DI III ยังทำลายอยู่. Treatment: very high-dose LT4 + add LT3 (liothyronine) ± propranolol; เมื่อ hemangioma regresses → normalize. Huang SA NEJM 2000 = reference.
Q: ทารก Down syndrome NBS TSH 8 mU/L ที่ 3 วัน — จัดการอย่างไร?

ตอบ: TSH 6–10 mU/L = subclinical hypothyroidism ที่ต้องติดตาม. Down syndrome: prevalence CH สูงกว่า 28–35×. Action: recheck serum TSH + fT4 ที่ 2–4 weeks. Claret 2013: 73.6% subclinical CH ใน Down syndrome resolves spontaneously. ถ้า TSH ยังสูง (>10 mU/L) หรือ T4/fT4 ต่ำ → start LT4 ตาม standard CH guidelines. ถ้า TSH ปกติ → no treatment แต่ monitor. Long-term: ตรวจ TSH ทุก 6–12 months ตลอดชีวิต (เสี่ยง autoimmune thyroid disease เพิ่มขึ้นตามอายุ).
Q: Central CH ต่างจาก primary CH อย่างไร ใน labs? ทำไมต้อง check adrenal ก่อน treat?

ตอบ: Primary CH: low T4/fT4 + HIGH TSH. Central CH: low T4/fT4 + normal or LOW TSH. Central: เกิดจาก pituitary/hypothalamic dysfunction → มัก associated panhypopituitarism (GH↓, ACTH↓, gonadotropin↓). LT4 เพิ่ม metabolic rate → ↑ cortisol clearance → adrenal insufficiency crisis ถ้า ACTH/cortisol reserve ต่ำ. ดังนั้น: confirm cortisol/ACTH adequate ก่อน; ถ้า uncertain → hydrocortisone stress-dose cover ขณะ start LT4.
Q: Pendred syndrome: gene, triad, และ NBS detection — ทำไม NBS มักพลาด?

ตอบ: Gene: SLC26A4 (pendrin) on chr 7; AR; expressed in cochlea, thyroid, kidney. Triad: partial iodine organification defect + goiter + sensorineural hearing loss. Pendrin ใน thyroid: apical efflux of iodide. NBS พลาดเพราะ: thyroid phenotype usually mild; TSH ไม่ high enough ขณะ screen; goiter + hypothyroidism เกิดในภายหลัง (variable, depends on iodine intake). Hearing loss อาจเป็น clue ที่นำไปสู่ diagnosis (Pendred = Pendred syndrome, not the drug). Genetic testing สำคัญ.
Day 87 Preview

Avery Chapter 87 — Neonatal Hypoglycemia and Hyperglycemia
พรุ่งนี้: Neonatal Hypoglycemia and Hyperglycemia — หลังเรียน thyroid hormones วันนี้ (endocrine regulation ของ thyroid axis) พรุ่งนี้เราเรียน glucose homeostasis: อีก endocrine emergency ที่ NICU พบทุกวัน. Key topics: definition ของ neonatal hypoglycemia (<40 vs <47 mg/dL controversy), hyperinsulinism (KATP channel mutations, SUR1/KIR6.2), IDM, screening protocols, diazoxide vs octreotide vs surgery, และ neonatal hyperglycemia ใน VLBW.
